_comment: Trusted public DNS resolvers, known-malicious DNS infrastructure (APT28 FrostArmada), and common ISP DNS ranges. Sources: public resolver documentation, CISA AA24-038A (Feb 2024), FBI/CISA/NSA/NCSC AA26-097A (Apr 2026), Spamhaus, Shadowserver Foundation.

resolvers.name: Google Public DNS
resolvers.logging_policy: Limited — 48-hour temporary logs, anonymized after 24h per privacy policy
resolvers.notes: Most widely used public DNS globally. Reliable, fast anycast. Google logs DNS queries per privacy policy.

resolvers.name: Cloudflare DNS
resolvers.logging_policy: No query logging per policy; audited by KPMG annually
resolvers.notes: Privacy-first DNS. Fastest resolver in many benchmarks. Cloudflare's 1.1.1.1 for Families variants: 1.1.1.2 (malware blocking), 1.1.1.3 (malware + adult content blocking).

resolvers.name: Cloudflare DNS for Families (malware blocking)
resolvers.logging_policy: No query logging per policy
resolvers.notes: Blocks malware and phishing domains in addition to standard resolution.

resolvers.name: Quad9
resolvers.logging_policy: No IP address logging; aggregate statistics only; GDPR compliant under Swiss law
resolvers.threat_intelligence: Blocks malicious domains using feeds from 18+ threat intelligence partners including IBM X-Force, Abuse.ch, RiskIQ
resolvers.notes: Security-focused. Blocks domains associated with malware, phishing, botnets. Alternate non-blocking: 9.9.9.10 / 149.112.112.10

resolvers.name: OpenDNS Home
resolvers.logging_policy: Logs queries; used for threat intelligence and parental control features
resolvers.notes: Cisco-owned. Supports customizable content filtering via OpenDNS dashboard. FamilyShield variant: 208.67.222.123 / 208.67.220.123.

resolvers.name: OpenDNS FamilyShield
resolvers.logging_policy: Logs queries
resolvers.notes: Blocks adult content and malware. No account required.

resolvers.name: AdGuard DNS (Default — ads + trackers)
resolvers.logging_policy: No logging by default; optional for registered users
resolvers.notes: Blocks ads, trackers, and malware domains. Non-filtering variant: 94.140.14.140 / 94.140.14.141

resolvers.name: AdGuard DNS (Family Protection — ads + adult content)
resolvers.logging_policy: No logging by default
resolvers.notes: Blocks ads, trackers, adult content, and malware.

resolvers.name: CleanBrowsing Security Filter
resolvers.logging_policy: Anonymized aggregate logs only
resolvers.notes: Blocks malware, phishing, typosquatting. Family filter: 185.228.168.168 / 185.228.169.168. Adult filter: 185.228.168.10 / 185.228.169.11

resolvers.name: CleanBrowsing Family Filter
resolvers.logging_policy: Anonymized aggregate logs only
resolvers.notes: Blocks adult content, malware, and mixed-content HTTPS sites.

resolvers.name: NextDNS
resolvers.logging_policy: Configurable per account; logs retained max 30 days; can be disabled
resolvers.notes: Highly configurable privacy/security DNS. Per-account filtering with privacy analytics. Free tier available (300K queries/month). Specific configuration IPs assigned per account.

resolvers.name: Comodo Secure DNS
resolvers.logging_policy: Query logs retained for security analysis
resolvers.notes: Blocks malware and phishing domains. No DNS-over-HTTPS support. Anycast limited.

resolvers.name: Neustar UltraDNS (formerly Verisign Public DNS)
resolvers.logging_policy: Queries logged for security purposes
resolvers.notes: Previously marketed as Verisign Public DNS. Stable, DNSSEC-validating. Threat-protection variant: 156.154.70.2 / 156.154.71.2

resolvers.name: Yandex DNS (Basic)
resolvers.logging_policy: Queries logged per Yandex privacy policy
resolvers.notes: Russian provider. Not recommended for privacy-sensitive environments. Safe variant (malware/adult blocking): 77.88.8.88 / 77.88.8.2

resolvers.name: Yandex DNS (Safe — malware blocking)
resolvers.logging_policy: Queries logged per Yandex privacy policy
resolvers.notes: Blocks malware domains. Russian jurisdiction — not recommended for use in sensitive Western networks.

resolvers.name: DNS.WATCH
resolvers.logging_policy: No logging
resolvers.notes: Uncensored, no-log DNS from Germany. DNSSEC validating. Small provider with limited redundancy.

resolvers.name: Level3/CenturyLink (Lumen) Public DNS
resolvers.logging_policy: Unknown
resolvers.notes: Long-established public resolver on 4.2.2.x range. Not officially documented as a public service but widely used. Operated on backbone infrastructure.

resolvers.name: FreeDNS (Freie Nameserver)
resolvers.logging_policy: No logging claimed
resolvers.notes: Small Austrian free DNS provider. Limited redundancy.

resolvers.name: Hurricane Electric Free DNS
resolvers.logging_policy: Unknown
resolvers.notes: DNSSEC-validating. Hurricane Electric is a major Internet backbone provider.

resolvers.name: Cisco Umbrella (Enterprise)
resolvers.logging_policy: Full query logging for enterprise security analytics
resolvers.notes: Enterprise variant of OpenDNS. Requires Umbrella subscription for full threat blocking. Shares IPs with OpenDNS consumer product.

known_malicious_dns._comment: DNS infrastructure attributed to APT28 (GRU Unit 26165) FrostArmada campaign. Sources: CISA AA24-038A, FBI/CISA/NSA/NCSC AA26-097A, Spamhaus, Shadowserver. IPs in these ranges redirecting DNS should be treated as indicators of compromise on monitored networks.

specific_ips.role: Rogue DNS resolver — redirects banking and government domains to phishing infrastructure
Rogue DNS resolver — used for credential harvesting via DNS hijack on compromised TP-Link/MikroTik routers
Secondary rogue DNS — failover resolver in FrostArmada botnet architecture
Primary rogue DNS resolver — observed in PCAP captures from compromised EdgeRouter X nodes
DNS-over-TLS rogue resolver — evades standard DNS monitoring by using DoT port 853
Rogue DNS — specifically used in Ukrainian ISP targeting phase of FrostArmada

isp_common_dns._comment: Common ISP DNS servers that are legitimate but not in the well-known public resolver list. Presence of these as a device's configured DNS is normal and expected for devices on those ISP networks. Unexpected presence on a network not associated with that ISP could indicate misconfiguration or compromise.

ranges.notes: Primary Comcast recursive DNS. Normal for Comcast residential customers.
Charter/Spectrum DNS range (legacy Time Warner Cable infrastructure).
Widely used Level3 anycast DNS. Often set manually by users.
AT&T broadband primary DNS. Normal for AT&T residential customers.
EarthLink legacy ISP DNS. Rarely seen on modern networks.
